NASCA Uno.activeSince: Early 1990s birth near Munich; graffiti-to-mural path per artsmania.ca (2020)
NASCA Uno.heroHook: Nasca Uno / Armin E. Mendocilla (@nasca_one): STRAAT Museum collection text; artsmania.ca (2020) interview cites German-Peruvian roots and Berlin-based mural tours.
NASCA Uno.storyFullText:
STRAAT Museum’s collection database hosts a work statement emphasizing narrative characters in Nasca Uno’s murals.

Artsmania.ca (2020) interviews Armin E. Mendocilla about German and Peruvian heritage, comic influences, and international mural travel.

Wooster Collective archives an early feature useful for historical context.

Spelling variants Nasca Uno / NASCA Uno appear—match artist’s official caps on socials.
NASCA Uno.pressText:
Artsmania — 2020 — Interview — https://artsmania.ca/2020/02/17/interview-with-nasca-uno/
STRAAT Museum — — Collection database entry — https://straatmuseum.com/en/collection-database/nasca-uno

Animalitoland.activeSince: Digital illustration from 1999; street painting from 2011; full-time nomadic from 2014 per animalitoland.com bio
Animalitoland.heroHook: Argentine muralist Graciela Gonçalves Da Silva as Animalitoland (@animalitoland): animalitoland.com bio cites UBA design study, street painting from 2011, 60+ murals.
Animalitoland.storyFullText:
animalitoland.com/about outlines graphic-design training in Buenos Aires, early digital illustration, a 2011 pivot to murals, and full-time travel from 2014.

Press pdfs on the site reference Pictoplasma and book anthologies such as Street Art by Women.

Voyagela interview adds Los Angeles relocation context—verify current city on Instagram bio.

Creature-based acrylic language merges comics pacing with painterly texture.

Thibaud Herem.heroHook: French illustrator Thibaud Hérem (@thibaudherem): Handsome Frank bio; toffygreen.com feature on architectural ink drawing; Netflix Our Beloved Summer artwork noted on site.
Thibaud Herem.storyFullText:
Handsome Frank’s rep page summarizes Hérem’s hand-drawn architecture with cross-hatching and weeks-long production timelines.

thibaudherem.com/pages/about lists Hermès, Four Seasons, Nike, Samsung, Hyundai, and Netflix’s Our Beloved Summer among projects.

Toffygreen.com’s long feature narrates on-location drawing discipline.

He publishes books with Nobrow, Cicada, and Laurence King—verify ISBNs before citing.
Thibaud Herem.pressText:
Toffygreen — — Feature — https://toffygreen.com/en/thibaud-herem-feature/
Handsome Frank — — Rep bio — http://handsomefrank.com/illustrators/thibaud-herem

Jennypo Art.heroHook: Tattooist-illustrator Jenny Pokryvailo (@jennypoart): jennypoart.com states black-and-white essence focus; stippling brush product on Gumroad—verify exhibition list separately.
Jennypo Art.storyFullText:
jennypoart.com introduces Jenny Pokryvailo’s black-and-white illustration and tattoo practice focused on contrast and reduction.

Gumroad sells stippling brush sets credited to jennypoart.

Threads mirrors @jennypoart with tattoo and Procreate workflow notes.

Confirm city and studio name from Instagram bio before printing packaging.

Mathew Gagnon.heroHook: Montreal artist Matthew Gagnon (@mattjgag): matthewgagnonart.com cites urban geometry in acrylic and pen; Elaine Fleck Toronto show Sept 2023 listed on site.
Mathew Gagnon.storyFullText:
matthewgagnonart.com/contact uses mattjgag@gmail.com and summarizes urban landscape painting with acrylic and technical pen.

The site lists 2023 exhibitions in Toronto and Bordeaux.

Instagram documents alley studies around Montreal neighborhoods—good for location-specific storytelling.

Street Collector uses spelling Mathew Gagnon—align with artist preference.

Rik Lee.heroHook: Melbourne illustrator Rik Lee (@rikleeillustration): riklee.net/about lists Amex, Virgin Australia, Stüssy; exhibitions in Melbourne, Sydney, LA, NYC.
Rik Lee.storyFullText:
riklee.net/about lists collaborations with American Express, Virgin Australia, Ray Ban, Air New Zealand, Stüssy, Vice, and Laurence King Publishing.

He notes exhibitions across Melbourne, Sydney, Bali, Los Angeles, San Francisco, and New York.

Linktree routes to shops and socials.

Personal interests (surfing, dogs) appear on the about page—optional flavor for lifestyle copy.

Paola Delfin.heroHook: Mexican muralist Paola Delfín Gaytán (@paola_delfin): Wikipedia and Global Muralism cite 1989 CDMX birth, monochrome figurative walls on several continents.
Paola Delfin.storyFullText:
Spanish Wikipedia and Mexico’s Secretaría de Cultura semblanza agree on a 1989 Mexico City birth and self-taught mural trajectory within post-graffiti figuration.

Global Muralism’s essay describes monochrome, illustration-like women intertwined with botanicals and community research.

She lists Nike, Converse, and Sharpie among collaborators on Wikipedia—verify with press releases.

International mural cities include Kiev, Chennai, and European capitals per the same sources.
Paola Delfin.notes: Accent spelling Delfín in Spanish sources.

Wotto.heroHook: Illustrator Craig Watkins Wotto (@wotto76): wottoart.com About cites BA 2000, now Orange County CA; Disney, Star Wars, Crocs named in client list.
Wotto.storyFullText:
wottoart.com/about identifies Craig Watkins behind Wotto, graduating illustration BA in 2000, working from London then relocating to Orange County.

Client lists include Disney, Star Wars, Crocs, DreamWorks, Cartoon Network, Nintendo, and Universal style guides.

Dribbble and Linktree mirror the doodle-brand aesthetic.

AlwaysArt portfolio duplicates summaries—use official site as canonical.

Yippie Hey.heroHook: Hamburg studio YIPPIEHEY (@yippiehey): yippiehey.com/about names founder Jacob Eisinger; Apple, Nike, Adidas, McDonald’s on client page.
Yippie Hey.storyFullText:
yippiehey.com/about presents YIPPIEHEY as Jacob Eisinger’s Hamburg studio blending illustration, 3D, and animation.

Client grids list Apple, Nike, Adidas, McDonald’s, Mercedes, Adobe, and major magazines.

Representation spans Kombinat Rotweiss, Much Collab, and Much Amsterdam per the info page.

NFT-era projects appear—confirm current availability before marketing.

Keya Tama.activeSince: Born Cape Town 1997; mural travel with parent artist per SDVoyager (2019)
Keya Tama.heroHook: South African muralist Keya Tama (@keyatama): keyatama.com/bio cites Cape Town 1997 birth; Royal Delft museum page documents residency project context.
Keya Tama.storyFullText:
keyatama.com/bio describes a Cape Town-born artist exploring ancestral iconography with minimal graphic forms, now based in New York.

Royal Delft Museum published a residency/project page tying him to Delftware collaboration.

SDVoyager (2019) interview recounts early travel painting with a muralist parent and a Cartoon Network Studios wall in 2017.

Verify mural geography on Instagram before claiming specific cities.

SDVoyager — 2019 — Interview — https://sdvoyager.com/interview/meet-keya-tama/

Facio.activeSince: UNA art studies from 2013; born 1990 (Street Art Cities bio)
Facio.heroHook: Argentine muralist Tomás Facio (@tomas_facio): Street Art Cities bio cites Merlo 1990 birth, UNA studies from 2013; monochrome line murals in several countries.
Facio.storyFullText:
Street Art Cities summarizes Tomás Facio as a Merlo-born muralist who studied at UNA from 2013 with an engraving-influenced line language.

Barbara Picci’s blog documents 2024 work in Merlo with detailed process photography.

Festival Asalto (Zaragoza) press notes his 2024 wall referencing historic toys—verify quotes from Cadena SER article.

Use @tomas_facio per Barbara Picci caption research; avoid unverified alternate handles.
Facio.notes: User brief: Tomas Facio / street art profile.

Woizo.heroHook: French muralist Woizo (@woizoner): woizo.art biography cites Rennes origin, graphic-design training, graffiti route; Toulouse base on Artsper/Urbaneez listings.
Woizo.storyFullText:
woizo.art’s biography (French) traces Breton roots, graphic-design education, graffiti, and travels in Africa shaping color choices.

Artsper and Urbaneez seller copy describe Toulouse as a working base while noting Rennes origin.

street-artwork.com hosts an English profile with mural photography.

Big Cartel shop links appear on aggregator pages—confirm inventory on live store.

Thomas Stary.activeSince: Murals since 2010 (tomasstary.com); MA industrial design UWB Pilsen 2017–2020 (LinkedIn)
Thomas Stary.heroHook: Prague designer Tomáš Starý (@tomasstary.studio): tomasstary.com mural section since 2010; LinkedIn cites UI/UX plus MA industrial design in Pilsen.
Thomas Stary.storyFullText:
tomasstary.com presents mural painting since 2010 alongside graphic and digital product work.

tomasstary.cz and LinkedIn describe Prague-based practice spanning branding, UI/UX, and large-scale walls.

Contact emails and phone numbers are published on studio pages.

Match English name Thomas Stary to Czech Tomáš Starý for bilingual marketing.

Vivaladybug.heroHook: Illustrator Viva La Ladybug (@vivalaladybug): Instagram and GIPHY channel document hybrid character art and widely shared GIFs—limited formal CV URLs.
Vivaladybug.storyFullText:
Instagram posts such as the Maidmer hybrid print announcement show a long-running character-illustration practice.

GIPHY hosts a vivalaladybug channel with millions of views on animated stickers.

vivalaladybug.com is referenced on GIPHY—verify live domain content.

No LinkedIn or agency biography surfaced in the quick search pass.
Vivaladybug.notes: Sparse CV; confirm artist legal name before contracts.

Iain Macarthur.heroHook: London illustrator Iain Macarthur (@iain.macarthur): LinkedIn cites freelance since 2009; Nike, HBO, Wired named; Lürzer’s Archive 200 Best mention on directory site.
Iain Macarthur.storyFullText:
LinkedIn summarizes Macarthur’s surreal, pattern-rich pencil work for Nike, Pepsi, HBO, Johnnie Walker, Wired, and other clients since 2009.

iainmacarthur.com hosts portfolio and contact data.

Directory of Illustration notes Lürzer’s Archive 200 Best Illustrators selection for 2016/17.

Behance @iainmac remains a major archive with six-figure project views.

Igor Mikutski.storyFullText:
Search-indexed Instagram reels show large acrylic-on-paper paintings with figurative and conflict-themed hashtags.

No authoritative interview, gallery rep page, or personal domain was retrieved in the automated search pass.

Avoid inventing education, city, or exhibition history until a primary CV appears.

Use direct messages or gallery representation links from bio when available.
